Title: OsSTK-Mediated Sakuranetin Biosynthesis and Carbon Flux Orchestrate Growth and Defence in Rice

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: sakuranetin branch of flavonoid phytoalexin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes sakuranetin accumulation and is required for sucrose-enhanced blast resistance against Magnaporthe oryzae.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; sucrose treatment; pathogen infection assay; UPLC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; prior exogenous sakuranetin rescue evidence cited in paper
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by sucrose treatment and by Magnaporthe oryzae infection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by sucrose treatment and by Magnaporthe oryzae infection
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antifungal phytoalexin contributing to rice blast resistance
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, NCBI BioProject PRJNA1154465 and PRJNA1148223; metabolomics by widely targeted metabolomics and sugar metabolomics (no accession reported)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsNOMT was identified among sucrose-induced genes and encodes the enzyme that directly converts naringenin to sakuranetin. 2. Sucrose increased OsNOMT expression and sakuranetin accumulation. 3. osnomt mutants showed strongly reduced sucrose-conferred resistance to Magnaporthe oryzae, demonstrating that OsNOMT-dependent sakuranetin biosynthesis is essential for this defence response. 4. OsNOMT is a direct transcriptional target of OsSTK.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful pathway target to elevate sakuranetin-mediated blast resistance, especially in combination with upstream regulator OsSTK or carbon-allocation engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: OsNOMT promoter contains STKRE motifs bound by OsSTK in Y1H, EMSA, transient LUC and ChIP-qPCR assays.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: upstream phenylpropanoid entry step supplying flavonoid phytoalexin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: deaminates phenylalanine to cinnamate, supplying phenylpropanoid precursors for downstream defence metabolite biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively associated with OsSTK-mediated defence and likely contributes precursor supply for phytoalexin accumulation and blast resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; promoter LUC transactivation; ChIP-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports defence-related secondary metabolite biosynthesis and rice blast resistance
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, NCBI BioProject PRJNA1154465 and PRJNA1148223
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsPAL4 was identified as an OsSTK-upregulated defence-related gene. 2. Its promoter contains STKRE motif(s) and is directly activated by OsSTK in transient assays. 3. ChIP-qPCR confirmed OsSTK enrichment on the OsPAL4 promoter. 4. The paper positions OsPAL4 as part of the OsSTK regulon that promotes defence metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate stacking partner with OsSTK to strengthen phenylpropanoid-derived defence metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although directly regulated by OsSTK, the paper did not independently manipulate OsPAL4 genetically in this study; its role is inferred from known defence function and direct promoter activation.

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: japonica var. Nipponbare; natural haplotypes also surveyed across cultivated rice varieties
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: japonica var. Nipponbare; haplotype analysis also included Zs97, Mxz, Th101, Xfz, 9311, Ez5, Mtz, Ywsm and 356 rice genomes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: carbon allocation/partitioning, sucrose-associated signalling, phytoalexin biosynthesis, precursor supply, and growth-related metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates OsNOMT directly to promote sakuranetin biosynthesis; also activates OsPAL4, OsTPS8 and OsPP2C08 promoters.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential positive regulator linking sucrose signalling to sakuranetin accumulation and blast resistance while also promoting growth and yield.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases sucrose, sakuranetin, photosynthetic capacity, plant height, biomass and grain yield, and enhances resistance to Magnaporthe oryzae; knockout reduces sucrose-induced sakuranetin accumulation and disease resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H screen; subcellular localization; transient dual-LUC assays; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; overexpression; RT-qPCR; metabolomics; pathogen infection assay; photosynthesis measurement; haplotype association; protoplast complementation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by sucrose treatment and progressively induced during Magnaporthe oryzae infection
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by sucrose treatment and progressively induced during Magnaporthe oryzae infection
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: OsNOMT promoter STKRE; OsPAL4 promoter; OsTPS8 promoter; OsPP2C08 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: sucrose treatment; Magnaporthe oryzae infection; field growth conditions for yield evaluation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: sakuranetin functions as an antifungal phytoalexin for rice blast resistance
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, NCBI BioProject PRJNA1154465 and PRJNA1148223; sugar metabolomics; widely targeted metabolomics
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsSTK was identified by Y1H as a direct transcription factor targeting the OsNOMT promoter through STKRE motifs. 2. OsSTK is induced by sucrose and pathogen infection, and is required for sucrose-induced OsNOMT expression, sakuranetin accumulation and blast resistance. 3. Overexpression of OsSTK increases photosynthesis, carbon assimilation, soluble sugars, sakuranetin and other defence metabolites while also improving plant height and grain yield. 4. OsSTK directly activates OsPAL4, OsTPS8 and OsPP2C08, indicating coordinated control of defence and growth. 5. Natural OsSTK haplotypes differ functionally; OsSTK34R81M is transcriptionally active whereas OsSTK34H81L is largely inactive in promoter activation assays and protoplast tests.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No clear growth penalty reported; instead OsSTK overexpression improved growth and yield while enhancing defence. Mature seed total protein, amino acid, fatty acid, amylose, flavonoid and soluble sugar contents were unchanged relative to wild type.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for breeding and engineering rice with both enhanced blast resistance and improved yield; prioritize functional OsSTK34R81M allele or activation of endogenous OsSTK, potentially stacked with OsNOMT or other downstream targets.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper reports five major haplotypes and emphasizes two functional protein variants, OsSTK34R81M and OsSTK34H81L, whose amino acids at positions 34 and 81 determine transcriptional activity. The Discussion contains an apparent inconsistency on which haplotype associates with higher resistance, but Results data support OsSTK34R81M as the active promoter-activating form.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for OsSTK overexpression; native target promoters assayed in transient systems

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Nipponbare-like haplotype; observed in Zs97, Mxz, Th101 and Xfz
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: osstk-2 protoplasts; cultivated rice accessions and N. benthamiana transient assay
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: STOREKEEPER family transcription factor variant with R34/M81 residues
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: sucrose-responsive defence and growth gene activation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates promoters of OsNOMT, OsPAL4, OsTPS8 and OsPP2C08; increases their expression in osstk-2 protoplasts.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Functional active OsSTK allele that determines stronger sakuranetin-associated immunity in high-sucrose rice cultivars.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Confers stronger activation of sakuranetin biosynthesis-associated genes and stronger blast-resistance-associated transcriptional response than OsSTK34H81L.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Dual-LUC transient expression assays; protoplast transformation/complementation; haplotype sequencing and association analysis; RT-qPCR after infection
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Comparable mRNA level to OsSTK34H81L under basal and infection conditions
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Comparable mRNA level to OsSTK34H81L under basal and infection conditions
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: OsNOMT promoter; OsPAL4 promoter; OsTPS8 promoter; OsPP2C08 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Magnaporthe oryzae infection; transient expression assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsSTK34R81M is the transcriptionally active OsSTK allele identified from natural haplotypes. 2. It activates OsNOMT, OsPAL4, OsTPS8 and OsPP2C08 promoters in dual-LUC assays. 3. It restores/increases target gene expression in osstk-2 protoplasts. 4. This allele underlies stronger sakuranetin-mediated immunity among high-sucrose cultivars.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Preferred allele for marker-assisted selection or allele replacement/editing to improve both blast resistance and yield traits.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Defined by amino acids R34 and M81 caused by exon SNPs at nucleotide positions 101 and 241.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: SK effector construct in transient assays

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: observed in 9311, Ez5, Mtz and Ywsm
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: osstk-2 protoplasts; cultivated rice accessions and N. benthamiana transient assay
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: STOREKEEPER family transcription factor variant with H34/L81 residues
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: loss or strong reduction of transcriptional activation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: sucrose-responsive defence and growth gene activation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Fails to effectively activate promoters of OsNOMT, OsPAL4, OsTPS8 and OsPP2C08 in tested assays.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Inactive/weak OsSTK allele associated with lower induction of OsNOMT after infection and weaker sakuranetin-associated immunity relative to OsSTK34R81M.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Shows little or no activation of sakuranetin biosynthesis-associated genes in dual-LUC and protoplast assays.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Dual-LUC transient expression assays; protoplast transformation/complementation; haplotype sequencing and association analysis; RT-qPCR after infection
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Comparable mRNA level to OsSTK34R81M under basal and infection conditions
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Comparable mRNA level to OsSTK34R81M under basal and infection conditions
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: OsNOMT promoter; OsPAL4 promoter; OsTPS8 promoter; OsPP2C08 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Magnaporthe oryzae infection; transient expression assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsSTK34H81L is a naturally occurring OsSTK allele with reduced transcriptional activity. 2. It does not substantially activate OsNOMT, OsPAL4, OsTPS8 or OsPP2C08 promoters in dual-LUC assays. 3. It fails to increase target gene expression in osstk-2 protoplasts. 4. This functional divergence explains variable sakuranetin accumulation and blast resistance among high-sucrose rice cultivars.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Allele to avoid in breeding for strong OsSTK-dependent defence/growth benefits; candidate for corrective editing to active residues R34/M81.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Defined by amino acids H34 and L81 caused by two nonsynonymous exon SNPs.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: SK effector construct in transient assays
